content.description:
India is home to the second largest number of diabetes patients in the world, with more than half remaining undiagnosed. Distance, cost, and lack of resources prevents millions of people from accessing diagnostic blood tests. However, over a billion people living in India have mobile phones, which now can be used to save their lives. 

Voice to Diabetes is an innovative tool that leverages AI to diagnose type 2 diabetes based on the analysis of a person's voice. Traditionally, Type 2 diabetes diagnosis involves invasive blood work, but this study introduces a non-invasive and efficient alternative. Voice to Diabetes harnesses AI and machine learning techniques to detect subtle vocal changes that are imperceptible to the human ear. By incorporating features like pitch and intensity changes, the tool successfully distinguishes between individuals with and without Type 2 diabetes. 

The study, published in a Mayo Clinic journal, involved 267 participants in India, with 192 without diabetes and 75 with a prior diagnosis. Using a smartphone app, participants recorded short voice clips multiple times daily for two weeks. We analyzed 18,465 recordings, identifying 14 different acoustic characteristics that led to the creation of a highly accurate diabetes identification tool. For women, changes in pitch and deviation from the average pitch were crucial, while for men, the diagnosis relied on variations in voice strength or amplitude. With an accuracy of up to 89% for women and 86% for men, this innovative approach could pave the way for an accurate, low-cost, and accessible test for diabetes.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Voice 2 Diabetes is an AI-powered mobile tool that non-invasively diagnoses Type 2 diabetes through voice analysis, offering an accessible alternative to traditional blood tests for millions in India.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: People in India at risk of or living with undiagnosed Type 2 diabetes, particularly those facing barriers like distance, cost, or lack of resources for traditional blood tests.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: The primary tactic involves an audio-based diagnostic tool accessible via mobile phones, where users submit voice samples for AI analysis to detect Type 2 diabetes.
ai_enrichment.objective: To increase the diagnosis rate of Type 2 diabetes in India by providing an accessible, non-invasive, and cost-effective diagnostic method.

content.description: Bernie Wagenblast, the iconic voice of the New York City subway system, recently came out as a transgender woman. To celebrate Transgender Day of Visibility, we replaced her famous announcements with new ones, recorded in her female voice. For the first time ever, New Yorkers riding the subway were guided by a trans voice –and learned that transgender people are a part of their everyday lives.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign replaced the iconic New York City subway announcements with newly recorded versions by Bernie Wagenblast, now in her female voice, after she publicly came out as a transgender woman.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: The general public in New York City, specifically subway riders, to foster understanding and acceptance of transgender individuals.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: The campaign was executed by replacing the standard audio announcements across the New York City subway system with new recordings of Bernie Wagenblast's voice, reflecting her female identity.
ai_enrichment.objective: To increase visibility, normalize the presence, and foster acceptance of transgender people within everyday public life among New Yorkers.

content.description:
Magnetic Stories transforms the audio of scary Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) machines, which can be louder than a military jet, into beautiful children’s audiobooks.

With Magnetic Stories, Siemens Healthineers, a top MRI manufacturer, changes the way that hospitals, radiologists, parents, and children interact with their product. It is no longer a giant, cold, scary machine. Instead, it is a gateway to a delightful experience for children, allowing pediatric patients to immerse themselves in original stories during their scans, all while easing their fears about the MRI machines and contributing to their overall health journey.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Siemens Healthineers transforms the intimidating and loud sounds of MRI machines into engaging children's audiobooks, creating a positive and immersive experience for young patients during their scans.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Hospitals, radiologists, parents of pediatric patients, and children undergoing MRI scans.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Integrating original children's audiobooks, narrated by the transformed sounds of the MRI machine, directly into the patient experience during scans.
ai_enrichment.objective: To enhance product perception, alleviate pediatric patients' fears during MRI scans, and improve the overall patient experience, reinforcing Siemens Healthineers' position as an empathetic innovator.

content.description:
[World’s First Film Shot by a Car]

In an era of ad skepticism where people avoid advertisements at all costs, Hyundai Motor Company broke away from advertising norms. Instead, it focused on creating entertainment content that consumers could willingly experience and enjoy. 「Night Fishing」 is a short film shot entirely using 7 IONIQ car cameras. It was officially screened nationwide with a revolutionary $1 ticket for its 13-minute runtime. Consumers, drawn to its high quality, gladly paid to watch the ad in theaters. The resulting buzz not only generated unprecedented PR success but also significantly boosted IONIQ's brand preference.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: To circumvent ad skepticism, Hyundai created 'Night Fishing,' a high-quality short film shot entirely using IONIQ car cameras, distributed as paid entertainment in theaters for a $1 ticket.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Consumers who appreciate innovative cinematic experiences and are receptive to unconventional brand engagement, particularly those averse to traditional advertising and potential car buyers interested in unique brand experiences.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A 13-minute short film, 'Night Fishing,' was produced entirely with 7 IONIQ car cameras. It was then nationally screened in theaters with a revolutionary $1 ticket price, turning an ad into a paid entertainment event.
ai_enrichment.objective: To significantly boost IONIQ's brand preference and generate unprecedented PR success by creating engaging, consumer-willingly-experienced content.

content.description:
Through the "Better than New" initiative, Philips wants to focus more attention on selling refurbished products in a forward-looking manner. Well over 10 million gifts ended up in landfills in 2023 and whilst Philips is trying to reduce their environmental impact by refurbishing returned products, the initiative is aimed at highlighting the problems within society and transforming the way people buy and how retailers sell to consumers. 


The creative thinking behind the campaign focuses on the tension between ‘new equals better’ and ‘better than new’. Responding to the needs of an increasing number of consumers, and building on the company’s commitment to sustainability and driving a more circular economy, Philips’ goal is to make products that can be refurbished and reused.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign challenges the 'new equals better' mentality by positioning refurbished products as 'better than new', aiming to highlight societal waste and transform consumer buying habits towards a circular economy.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Environmentally conscious consumers, individuals prioritizing sustainability, and those open to challenging traditional consumption patterns regarding 'new' products.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: The campaign utilizes the 'Better than New' initiative to promote the sale of refurbished products, engaging in retail transformation and consumer education to shift buying and selling behaviors.
ai_enrichment.objective: To drive sales of Philips' refurbished products, reduce environmental impact, and foster a more circular economy by transforming consumer perceptions and purchasing habits.

content.description: Did you know 1 in 12 sheep is gay? Since they won’t breed, these rams are often killed. With wool prices at an all-time low, we found a way to create a new revenue stream for Sheep Farm Stücke and help gay sheep and gay people at the same time: Rainbow Wool is the first gay textile thread for queer rights worldwide. We rescued gay rams, giving them a safe space to live, love and grow wool. Their wool is crafted into fashion and was launched by German queer icon and Tokio Hotel frontman Bill Kaulitz, helping spark a global conversation about equality.  All profits fund global LGBTQ+ charities.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Rainbow Wool transforms wool from rescued gay rams into a unique fashion product, creating a sustainable revenue stream for a sheep farm while simultaneously funding LGBTQ+ charities and sparking global conversations about equality.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Globally-conscious consumers, LGBTQ+ allies, and fashion enthusiasts who value ethical products and support queer rights, including fans of Bill Kaulitz.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: The campaign involved rescuing gay rams to produce the unique 'Rainbow Wool' textile, which was then crafted into fashion and launched by German queer icon Bill Kaulitz to generate global media attention and discussion around LGBTQ+ equality.
ai_enrichment.objective: To establish a new, sustainable revenue stream for Schäferei Stücke, raise global awareness for queer rights and equality, and generate profits to fund LGBTQ+ charities.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign introduces 'Marina Prieto' to humanize JCDecaux's brand and demonstrate the creative effectiveness and strategic value of their Out-of-Home (OOH) advertising solutions. It aims to showcase how OOH can deliver impactful results for businesses.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Marketing and media decision-makers, including marketing directors, media planners, and brand managers at companies and advertising agencies, who are seeking effective advertising solutions.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: The campaign likely features a content series, such as video interviews or articles profiling 'Marina Prieto,' distributed across JCDecaux's digital channels, professional social media, and industry trade publications. This might also involve direct outreach and B2B events.
ai_enrichment.objective: To strengthen JCDecaux's B2B brand perception, educate potential clients on the creative impact of OOH advertising, and generate new leads and foster deeper client relationships.

content.description: Price Packs is the first brand that’s all about the price. Because the packaging design focuses entirely on it. Considering the usual packaging production requires several months, this becomes an irrevocable commitment to price stability. Placed next to competitors, the design helps consumers immediately spot the best prices. Price Packs are available in all 2100 Penny markets in Germany, promoted by an OOH campaign as striking as the packaging.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Penny's "Price Packs" campaign introduces a unique product packaging design that exclusively highlights the price, serving as a visual commitment to price stability and allowing consumers to instantly identify the best deals.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Price-conscious grocery shoppers across Germany seeking transparent and competitive pricing on everyday products.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: The campaign's primary tactic involved distinctive product packaging that boldly showcases prices, complemented by a striking Out-of-Home (OOH) advertising campaign across all 2100 Penny markets in Germany.

ai_enrichment.target_audience: Media agencies, advertisers, brands, and decision-makers in companies seeking Out-of-Home (OOH) advertising solutions from JCDecaux.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Tactics likely include a multi-channel approach leveraging JCDecaux's own Out-of-Home inventory, digital B2B platforms like LinkedIn, industry publications, and direct client communications.

content.description: Being around for over 150 years and present in 192 countries makes Heineken one of the most popular beer brands in the world, but most importantly: the most misspelled beer brand in the world. With millions of people all over the planet constantly mistreating our brand by mispronouncing, misspelling, and misusing Heineken. And so when we found out that people think Heineken is German, that nobody can spell nor pronounce our name and basically they don't care about our brand, we soon realized that those were not setbacks but proof of the brand's widespread enjoyment across cultures.  Afterall as our founder once said, we are in the business of good times. So instead of fighting these mistakes, we decided to celebrate them. Globally. And so for Heineken 150th anniversary, the brand decided to… not celebrate Heineken, but the way people enjoyed it. One way or another. And just like that, for a full year, we stopped being Heineken and started being Whateverken people wanted us to be, to have good times. Parties and events, tons of exclusive collabs with some of the most famous brands in the world (like... Adidas and Monopoly!), millions of bottles available all over the world, and lots of good times... and guess what? None of them spelled Heineken correctly. But who cares, we're in the good times business after all.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Heineken celebrates its 150th anniversary by embracing and turning common mispronunciations and misspellings of its name into a global testament to its widespread enjoyment and accessibility, rather than fighting them.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Global beer consumers of legal drinking age who casually interact with the Heineken brand and appreciate a lighthearted approach to brand identity.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: The campaign will involve a global celebration across various channels, likely including digital and social media activations, to highlight and humorously acknowledge the diverse ways people misspell and mispronounce 'Heineken'.
ai_enrichment.objective: To enhance brand affinity and reinforce Heineken's identity as a globally loved and accessible brand by celebrating authentic, often humorous, consumer interactions.

content.description: Japan is the only country with a law requiring married couples to share the same surname, with 95% of women adopting their husband's name. Changing the law is challenging, especially with 90% of lawmakers being men. Asuniwa, a leading gender equality organization, partnered with Tohoku University’s professor to project the law's future impact, concluding that by 2531, "Sato" would be the only surviving surname in Japan. To spark conversation, 40 entities—companies, artists, manga characters, and athletes—renamed themselves "SATO." Armed with this data, Asuniwa shared the findings with UN Women, leading to a recommendation for Japan to revise the law within two years. A candidate advocating for change was then elected prime minister.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: To highlight the future impact of Japan's surname law, the campaign projected 'Sato' as the only surviving surname by 2531, then mobilized 40 entities to temporarily rename themselves 'SATO' to spark public conversation and pressure for legal reform.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Japanese citizens (especially those open to legislative reform and gender equality issues), Japanese lawmakers, and international human rights and gender equality organizations like UN Women.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: The campaign used a data-driven projection from Tohoku University, orchestrated a symbolic name change by 40 diverse entities across various sectors, and employed strategic advocacy by Asuniwa with UN Women to influence policy.
ai_enrichment.objective: To raise public awareness, spark conversation, and ultimately influence legislative reform of Japan's surname law to allow married couples to retain separate surnames.

content.description:
When you think of Spotify ads, what comes to mind? Ads in between songs? During podcasts? It’s safe to say that video ads aren’t your first thought. And that has consequences for our ad revenue. Media planners bucket us as an audio-only platform, an incremental element  in the larger media plan.

So, to make Spotify’s video ad offerings unforgettable, we hacked the most analytical part of the media planning experience: the RFP spreadsheet. We created Spreadbeats, the first-ever music video coded inside an Excel spreadsheet. Millions of data points transformed into a stunning music video, proving that Spotify’s video ads can break through the best.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: To make Spotify's video ad offerings unforgettable, the campaign hacked the analytical RFP spreadsheet by coding a music video called 'Spreadbeats' directly within Excel, transforming millions of data points into a visual demonstration of their video capabilities.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Media planners, media buyers, and advertising professionals responsible for media allocation and campaign planning within the B2B advertising industry.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: The primary tactic involved creating and likely distributing or demonstrating an interactive music video, 'Spreadbeats,' entirely coded and playable within an Excel spreadsheet, directly targeting the tools and processes of media planners.
ai_enrichment.objective: To change the perception of Spotify among media planners from an audio-only platform to a recognized and competitive player in video advertising, ultimately increasing consideration for their video ad inventory.

content.description:
Lap of Legends redefined sports entertainment by blending cutting-edge technology and storytelling to create the first-ever real-vs.-virtual Formula 1 race. Starring Logan Sargeant at the iconic Silverstone Circuit, the project brought to life augmented reality avatars of legendary F1 drivers who raced against Logan in real time. Powered by advanced AI and machine learning, the virtual competitors were designed using 45 years of historical race data and 42.3 million data points, ensuring their driving styles and strategies mirrored their real-world counterparts.

Using real-time telemetry from Logan’s car, the digital avatars responded dynamically to his every move, creating an authentic and immersive race experience. The production captured this groundbreaking event with 28 unique camera angles—more than any F1 broadcast—providing viewers with an unprecedented perspective on the action. The film combined high-fidelity visuals, seamless AR integration, and cinematic storytelling to deliver an experience that was as exhilarating as it was innovative.

Lap of Legends was not just a race; it was a spectacle that bridged the gap between the physical and digital worlds. By leveraging state-of-the-art technology, Michelob ULTRA created an immersive narrative that resonated with fans globally, setting a new benchmark for innovation in sports entertainment.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Lap of Legends created the first-ever real-vs.-virtual Formula 1 race, where Logan Sargeant competed against augmented reality avatars of legendary F1 drivers. These virtual competitors were powered by AI and machine learning, meticulously designed using historical race data to mimic their real-world driving styles.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Adult Formula 1 and motorsport enthusiasts, particularly those interested in technology, innovation, and a premium, active lifestyle.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: The campaign staged a groundbreaking live event featuring AR and AI technology for a real-vs.-virtual race, which was captured with 28 unique cameras for subsequent multi-platform content distribution.
ai_enrichment.objective: To enhance brand awareness, drive engagement, and reinforce Michelob ULTRA's association with innovation, premium sports, and technology.

content.description:
Blood-Aid: A fast, new, cost effective and scalable way to reveal people's blood type.
Blood-Aid was developed to address the fact that 40% of Brazilians don't know their blood type. This lack of knowledge holds Brazilians back from addressing some health precautions that are related to people's blood type as well as to answer to blood donation calls for specific blood types.

PROBLEM: In Brazil, 40% of the population is unaware of their blood type. Knowing your blood type is important for several reasons. First, because some health factors are more common to specific blood types. Second, because when you do know your blood type you can donate when your specific blood type is in short supply and in need.
OPORTUNITY: As a health diagnostics and integrated health system provider DASA Diagnostics decided to make the blood type information be readily available to the patients, and also invite them to learn more about what their blood type means for their health and also to sign up for alerts of when their specific blood type was in need of donations.
IDEA: We developed a new kind of band-aid, which has a thin layer that in seconds when in touch with blood can detect what type of blood that is. Making the information of blood type readily available to everyone who comes for blood exams. This is Blood-Aid™. 
With that information, the patient is given specific literature developed by DASA concerning health traits of their specific blood type. Also, the patient is invited to sign up for alerts from the blood bank when their type of blood is in short supply and donations are needed.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A fast, cost-effective, and scalable diagnostic design solution called Blood-Aid aims to reveal people's blood types, addressing the significant portion of Brazilians unaware of theirs.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Brazilians, especially the 40% of the population unaware of their blood type, and public health organizations focused on preventative care and blood donation.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: The primary execution involves the widespread deployment and integration of the 'Blood-Aid' diagnostic method itself as a new product/service within healthcare systems, leveraging its design for accessibility.
ai_enrichment.objective: To increase the proportion of Brazilians who know their blood type, improving individual health management and the country's capacity for targeted blood donations.

metadata.title: Be Seen By The Best Eyes

content.description:
In today’s world, getting noticed has never been easier. Views, likes—whatever a retweet is called now—it’s a veritable buffet of empty calories for personal validation. But when it's just peers and parents tapping the like button, the creative sea can start to feel pretty shallow.

To remind the creative community why real recognition matters, we crafted a series of surreal, visually striking animated shorts—equal parts humor, absurdity, and eye-catching design. These films bring to life the idea of “Be Seen by the Best Eyes”—a metaphor for the discerning gaze of the ADC Awards jury, the industry’s tastemakers who don’t just see great work, but elevate it.

With endless platforms to showcase creativity, we needed to make the ADC Awards stand out as the pinnacle of creative recognition. The jurors chosen for this festival are the ones who shape careers, define what’s next, and are eager to discover work that genuinely moves the needle. This campaign was an open invitation to creators everywhere: bring your best work forward and get it seen by the most discerning eyes in the business.

To broaden our reach and inspire a more diverse range of submissions, we leveraged nostalgia—think Pixar meets ‘90s MTV—to deliver an impactful message with disruptive design. Because in a world full of noise, the best work makes it impossible to look away.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Surreal, humorous animated shorts metaphorically illustrate "Be Seen by the Best Eyes," representing the discerning gaze of the ADC Awards jury as the source of true creative recognition.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Creative professionals, including designers and art directors, seeking legitimate industry recognition beyond superficial online validation.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: The campaign employed a series of visually striking, surreal animated shorts distributed across various platforms to engage the target audience.
ai_enrichment.objective: To remind the creative community of the importance of real recognition and establish the ADC Awards as the definitive pinnacle of creative achievement.

content.description:
Every ad PEDIGREE makes around the world has one universal similarity. It always features a dog in it. We wondered, what if that dog could be adoptable. Partnering with Nexus Studios, we’ve developed a bespoke AI system that can transform a single image of a shelter dog into studio quality photography. This 'glow up' image is then mapped to a CGI rig, allowing the photo-real double to be positioned in any pose to suit any piece of PEDIGREE creative.   

From main meal, treat and retail ads, every dog we feature can now be a real shelter dog who needs a home.   

Adoptable places local shelter dogs into the ads you see, and when that dog is adopted it’s instantly dropped out of media rotation. The Adoptable initiative supercharges our brand purpose. Now every dollar we spend on media doesn’t just take our products to the world, it takes shelter dogs, too.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: PEDIGREE uses a bespoke AI system to transform images of local shelter dogs into studio-quality photography for all its advertisements, dynamically featuring adoptable dogs across various creative platforms.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Dog owners and potential dog owners who are considering adopting a pet, as well as the broader public exposed to PEDIGREE advertising.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Implementing AI and CGI technology to integrate photo-real doubles of adoptable shelter dogs into all PEDIGREE main meal, treat, and retail ads, with dynamic media rotation when a dog is adopted.
ai_enrichment.objective: To supercharge PEDIGREE's brand purpose by promoting dog adoption through its media spend and advertising, while reinforcing its commitment to dog welfare.

ai_enrichment.target_audience: Global citizens, sports enthusiasts, international media, and attendees in Paris, interested in cultural celebrations and major international events across all age groups.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A large-scale live experience and televised global broadcast, incorporating artistic performances, cultural displays, and traditional ceremonial elements to mark the official start of the Olympic Games.

content.description:
In the UK, football is our most popular national sport. It has an almost religious following with fans constantly playing, discussing, arguing and dreaming about the game. Bromley FC are in the National League which is a highly competitive league but without the big budgets of the Premier League.  

 

National League sides struggle to hire top talent as bigger, richer Premier League clubs hire the best. Top-tier football is currently being transformed by the increased use of data to win games, with teams of tacticians who use data to inform tactical decisions. But lower league teams are struggling to innovate.  

 

Football Manager is an award-winning football simulation game series that started in 1982. It’s the world’s most advanced football simulator, using real world football data collected by over 2000 scouts across every football league. The game gives you an unprecedented suite of data that only top managers would have access to, helping gamers analyse everything from XG (expected goals) to historical data going back 20 years. In FM you play as a manager and control tactics rather than controlling the players actions directly as in FIFA.  

 

Xbox champions the power of gaming under their current tagline ‘Power Your Dreams’ and in this case they were able to power gamers’ dreams to work in pro football and Bromley FC’s dream to challenge for promotion.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Leverage the advanced data analytics and tactical decision-making capabilities of the Football Manager game to provide real-world tactical support to Bromley FC, a National League team, helping them innovate and improve despite budget constraints.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: UK football fans aged 16-45, particularly those who play football simulation games like Football Manager, appreciate tactical insights in football, and follow lower league clubs.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Implement a real-world experience/activation where the tactical expertise and data systems from Football Manager are integrated with Bromley FC's coaching staff to influence real game strategy, likely documented through content across digital and sports media channels.
ai_enrichment.objective: To enhance brand perception and engagement for Football Manager (and XBOX) by demonstrating the game's real-world tactical depth and innovation, connecting with the passion of UK football fans and showcasing the practical application of the simulation.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A humorous campaign leveraging actor Michael Cera's name to playfully suggest he is the founder or deeply integrated with the CeraVe skincare brand, creating a memorable and unexpected association.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Younger adults and skincare enthusiasts (18-45) who appreciate humor, internet culture, and effective yet accessible skincare products.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film featuring Michael Cera, distributed across digital platforms like YouTube and social media, potentially supported by shorter video ads and influencer content.
ai_enrichment.objective: Generate significant brand awareness and buzz through a memorable, humorous celebrity partnership, increasing engagement and brand recall for CeraVe.

content.description:
In 2019, Marta Sodano, an Italian woman with Down syndrome, shared these words during a speech at the UN. "I discovered that in psychology there is a concept called “self-fulfilling prophecy”, whereby a teacher who thinks that a student cannot understand would just act accordingly and therefore they would not teach the student. And there you go: the prophecy self-fulfills." With these words, Marta described the hurdles she faced in her school experience. The self-fulfilling prophecy is a sociological and psychological concept, first described in 1948 by the US sociologist Robert K. Merton, illustrating how people’s assumptions and expectations affect events to such an extent that the initial prophecy comes true. Inspired by Marta’s words, we launched a campaign aimed at smashing assumptions from every angle.
In our launch film, a young woman with Down syndrome challenges the low expectations others have of her and proposes a shift in perspective. Initially, those around believe that she cannot achieve important goals. Then, halfway through the film, there’s a twist, and the protagonist forces the viewer – and society at large – to think outside the box. To break the stereotypes even further, we shared examples of prejudices that people with Down syndrome have shattered via our social media channels. We then invited the community to join us by using our official hashtag #AssumeThatICan

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Inspired by Marta Sodano's words on the 'self-fulfilling prophecy,' the campaign challenges societal assumptions and low expectations placed on individuals with Down syndrome.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: General public, including educators, parents, and anyone who might hold preconceived notions or assumptions about individuals with Down syndrome.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A hero film, likely distributed online and potentially via broadcast, serves as the primary channel to convey the campaign's message.
ai_enrichment.objective: To raise awareness and challenge negative assumptions and stereotypes about people with Down syndrome, fostering more inclusive expectations.

content.description:
In China, where pharmaceutical advertising is strictly regulated and sex remains a sensitive topic, promoting Viagra required a delicate yet compelling approach. So we turned intimacy into art—a medium that transcends restrictions while sparking conversation.
Viagra collaborated with a renowned artist, and invited three real couples to reflect on their experiences with fading passion and intimacy challenges. Through candid conversations, they confronted the reality of how long it had been since they last connected. Then, left alone with a camera, their moments of closeness enhanced by Viagra were captured through time-lapse photography—turning fleeting interactions into long lasting expressions of love.
A week later, the couples were invited to a private gallery to view their photography, reliving their emotions through art. Their stories and photographs were then released online on Chinese Valentine’s Day, driving organic discussions about intimacy while staying fully compliant with advertising regulations.
By leveraging art, real human stories, and cultural sensitivity, Viagra didn’t just sell performance—it rekindled desire in a way that felt authentic, emotional, and memorable.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign transformed fleeting moments of renewed intimacy, enhanced by Viagra, into lasting art through time-lapse photography, featuring real couples to subtly promote the product in a restrictive advertising environment.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Couples in China experiencing fading passion and intimacy challenges, likely middle-aged or older, who could benefit from Viagra but face cultural and advertising sensitivities.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Viagra collaborated with a renowned artist to capture real couples' renewed intimacy using time-lapse photography, after candid conversations about their challenges, culminating in a private gallery viewing of their 'art'. The medium is 'Film Craft', implying a film documenting this process.
ai_enrichment.objective: To increase brand awareness and destigmatize Viagra by reframing intimacy challenges as art, sparking conversation around the topic of lasting love and connection in a culturally sensitive and strictly regulated market.

content.description:
In Hot Water, created for WWF and launched at COP29, is a stop-motion film shot entirely on a thermal camera.

The film tackles the invisible crisis of ocean warming, raising awareness and inspiring urgent action against climate change.

How do you visualize something that cannot be seen? How do you convey the "invisible" yet devastating effects of ocean warming? By using a thermal camera, In Hot Water transformed heat into a vivid and tangible visual language.

Each frame required meticulous, frame-by-frame control of temperature, alongside the precise manipulation of 3D-printed replacement stop-motion models. State-of-the-art thermal cameras captured these details, while ovens, heat lamps, heat guns, and freezing sprays were used to achieve exact heat levels. Every stop-motion object in each frame was heated to separate temperatures, to within 0.1°C, creating vivid, layered visuals entirely in-camera. This painstaking process mirrored the fragility and urgency of marine ecosystems, turning the labor-intensive craft into a direct metaphor for the message: "every fraction of a degree matters."

"The process was a race against time: the moment a figure was removed from the oven, its temperature began falling rapidly. Using the thermal camera's sensors to monitor heat levels in real time, the team worked quickly to position and capture each figure before it cooled too much. If the process took too long, the scene had to be reheated and reset. The most challenging frames involved the boy and a fish overlapping, as the two objects required slightly different temperatures to maintain distinct colors. These frames took days to complete, with every detail calibrated for consistency across every consecutive frame of animation."

The narrative follows a young boy navigating an ocean heating to dangerous levels, symbolizing the catastrophic consequences of global warming. As the visuals evolve—from vibrant underwater life to a desolate, heat-scorched world—the film’s craft amplifies its emotional impact, immersing audiences in the critical need for climate action.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign visualizes the invisible crisis of ocean warming through a unique stop-motion film shot entirely with a thermal camera, transforming heat into a vivid and tangible visual language.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Global public, environmental advocates, and policymakers concerned with climate change and ocean warming, particularly those engaged with events like COP29.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A stop-motion film created using advanced thermal cameras, meticulously controlled temperature manipulation (ovens, heat lamps, freezing sprays), and 3D-printed models, launched at COP29.
ai_enrichment.objective: Raising awareness about the invisible crisis of ocean warming and inspiring urgent action against climate change.

content.description: Pubs are culturally significant pieces of Irish history, but many are now struggling to keep their doors open. The Irish Heritage Council offers protected status, tax support and other financial benefits to places of historical significance that are open to the public, such as museums. Heineken’s Pub Museums turns historic Irish pubs into virtual museums, making them eligible for tax reductions, helping their rich past safeguard their future.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Heineken's 'Pub Museums' initiative transforms struggling historic Irish pubs into virtual museums, allowing them to qualify for protected status and financial benefits from the Irish Heritage Council.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Owners and stakeholders of historic Irish pubs currently facing financial difficulties.

content.description:
Every ad PEDIGREE makes around the world has one universal similarity. It always features a dog in it. We wondered, what if that dog could be adoptable.Shape Partnering with Nexus Studios, we’ve developed a bespoke AI system that can transform a single image of a shelter dog into studio quality photography. This 'glow up' image is then mapped to a CGI rig, allowing the photo-real double to be positioned in any pose to suit any piece of PEDIGREE creative.   

From main meal, treat and retail ads, every dog we feature can now be a real shelter dog who needs a home.   

Adoptable places local shelter dogs into the ads you see, and when that dog is adopted it’s instantly dropped out of media rotation. The Adoptable initiative supercharges our brand purpose. Now every dollar we spend on media doesn’t just take our products to the world, it takes shelter dogs, too.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: PEDIGREE uses a bespoke AI system to transform images of local shelter dogs into studio-quality photos, allowing them to be featured as the main subject in all global brand advertising, with adopted dogs instantly removed from media rotation.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Current and prospective dog owners, as well as individuals interested in pet adoption and animal welfare, who are exposed to PEDIGREE's advertising.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: The campaign integrates a bespoke AI system with CGI technology to place photo-real versions of real shelter dogs into all forms of PEDIGREE's global advertising, including main meal, treat, and retail ads, across various media channels.
ai_enrichment.objective: To reinforce PEDIGREE's brand purpose and promote dog adoption by making every ad an adoption opportunity, thereby turning media spend into a platform for social good.

content.description:
Out-Of-Home Matches reinvented the use of outdoor media by transforming over 3,500 high-traffic OOH billboards into giant screens broadcasting UWCL matches in 13 cities. While OOH is a staple in media plans, it’s often used in conventional ways that fade into the background. Heineken broke this pattern by turning static billboards into a live entertainment activation, addressing the significant gender gap in football.

This innovative approach brought the concept of video—commonly explored in TV and internet advertising—to the streets, creating an impactful and engaging experience. Leveraging the high visibility, penetration, and affinity of OOH media among Heineken's target audience, the campaign directly connected with over 25 million people. The result? Women’s football became impossible to ignore, generating over R$ 52 million in earned media and turning a media channel into a cultural movement.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Heineken transformed 3,500 static outdoor billboards in 13 cities into live giant screens broadcasting UWCL matches, reinventing OOH media as a live entertainment activation to address the gender gap in football.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Urban populations and football enthusiasts in high-traffic areas across 13 cities, particularly those interested in or open to women's football, and Heineken's target consumers.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: The campaign transformed over 3,500 high-traffic OOH billboards across 13 cities into live giant screens to broadcast UWCL matches, creating a live entertainment activation that brought video content to the streets.
ai_enrichment.objective: To increase engagement and awareness for women's football through innovative outdoor media usage, while challenging traditional advertising norms and addressing the gender gap in sports.

content.description: Coca-Cola aims to recycle a bottle or can for each one they sell by 2030. The Coca-Cola logo is recognized by 94% of the world’s population. The objective was to use the brand’s famous identity to telegraph the importance of recycling in the most impactful way. So, we decided to break our very strict brand guidelines and crush Coca-Cola’s iconic logo to inspire people to recycle every single can.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The core creative idea involved intentionally breaking strict brand guidelines by crushing the universally recognized Coca-Cola logo on Out of Home placements to visually demonstrate the act and importance of recycling.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: General public and consumers of packaged beverages, particularly Coca-Cola products, who need to be inspired and educated on the importance of recycling.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: The campaign was executed through Out of Home (OOH) media, featuring imagery of Coca-Cola's iconic logo visibly crushed to represent the act of recycling.
ai_enrichment.objective: The objective was to use the brand’s famous identity to telegraph the importance of recycling in the most impactful way and inspire people to recycle every single can.

content.description: Japan is the only country with a law requiring married couples to share the same surname, with 95% of women adopting their husband's name. Changing the law is challenging, especially with 90% of lawmakers being men. Asuniwa, a leading gender equality organization, partnered with Tohoku University’s professor to project the law's future impact, concluding that by 2531, "Sato" would be the only surviving surname in Japan. To spark conversation, 40 entities—companies, artists, manga characters, and athletes—renamed themselves "SATO." Armed with this data, Asuniwa shared the findings with UN Women, leading to a recommendation for Japan to revise the law within two years. A candidate advocating for change was then elected prime minister.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign leveraged a projection that 'Sato' would be Japan's only surname by 2531 to dramatize the impact of the surname law, leading 40 entities to symbolically rename themselves 'SATO' to ignite public discourse.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Japanese citizens, lawmakers, and international gender equality organizations concerned with gender equality and legislative reform.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: It involved a partnership with Tohoku University to create a scientific projection, followed by a widespread public relations stunt where 40 diverse entities renamed themselves 'SATO', and direct advocacy with UN Women.
ai_enrichment.objective: To spark national conversation, shift public opinion, and influence legislative reform concerning Japan's surname law, ultimately aiming for its revision.

content.description: Dramamine, the #1 anti-nausea brand in the US, is so effective at preventing nausea that it ended the need for barf bags. That made us think: What if we reminded the world how effective Dramamine is by saying goodbye to the industry we are accidentally killing? So we set out to pay tribute to a fellow icon of nausea with The Last Barf Bag, an entire campaign built around giving the barf bag a grand farewell since Dramamine has barfing covered. We launched with a documentary that explores a every corner of the barf bag universe and premiered the film at a museum exhibit that literally put barf bags on a pedestal. We also designed an e-commerce platform that imagines a better future by selling repurposed bags for everything but barf—all to show people the impact and efficacy of Dramamine in an unexpected way.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Dramamine, asserting its effectiveness in preventing nausea, celebrates the 'retirement' of the barf bag with 'The Last Barf Bag' campaign, paying tribute to an icon it has rendered obsolete.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: General consumers who experience or are susceptible to nausea and motion sickness, and those interested in clever brand storytelling and efficacy demonstrations.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: The campaign launched with a documentary exploring the 'barf bag universe', premiered at a museum exhibit, and featured an e-commerce platform selling repurposed barf bags.
ai_enrichment.objective: Reinforce Dramamine's brand efficacy and leadership in preventing nausea, thereby driving brand awareness and consideration.

ai_enrichment.target_audience: Young adults and singles, or anyone feeling alienated or overwhelmed by the traditional romantic expectations of Valentine's Day.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Primarily utilized engaging content and interactive activations across social media platforms to promote the 'Erase Valentine's Day' concept and associated product.
ai_enrichment.objective: To increase brand engagement and relevance among its target demographic by offering a unique, relatable, and humorous take on a widely celebrated occasion.

content.description:
Magnetic Stories transforms the audio of scary Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) machines, which can be louder than a military jet, into beautiful children’s audiobooks.

With Magnetic Stories, Siemens Healthineers, a top MRI manufacturer, changes the way that hospitals, radiologists, parents, and children interact with their product. It is no longer a giant, cold, scary machine. Instead, it is a gateway to a delightful experience for children, allowing pediatric patients to immerse themselves in original stories during their scans, easing their fears about the MRI machines and contributing to their overall health journey.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Magnetic Stories transforms the loud, scary sounds of MRI machines into calming and engaging children's audiobooks, turning a potentially frightening medical procedure into a delightful and immersive experience for pediatric patients.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Pediatric patients and their parents, along with hospitals and radiologists who purchase and operate MRI machines.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: The campaign integrates custom audiobooks directly into MRI machines, utilizing the machine's sounds as part of the storytelling experience for children during their scans.
ai_enrichment.objective: To enhance brand perception, differentiate Siemens Healthineers' MRI machines, and improve the patient experience for pediatric scans, thereby influencing purchasing decisions in the B2B market.

content.description: Unsealed Books is an audio first solution that makes audio storytelling the key to solving a business problem. When a controversial Hungarian law censored physical books, we turned to audio storytelling to free the books from the government imposed wrapping paper and to provide access to literature to everyone.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Unsealed Books is an audio-first solution that utilizes audio storytelling to bypass controversial Hungarian censorship laws, making literature accessible despite government restrictions.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Hungarian citizens and literature enthusiasts impacted by censorship, seeking access to banned or restricted books.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: The campaign's primary tactic is creating and distributing audio storytelling content as an audio-first solution to free censored books.
ai_enrichment.objective: To provide unrestricted access to literature and circumvent censorship imposed by a controversial Hungarian law.

content.description:
India is home to the second largest number of diabetes patients in the world, with more than half remaining undiagnosed. Distance, cost, and lack of resources prevents millions of people from accessing diagnostic blood tests. However, over a billion people living in India have mobile phones, which now can be used to save their lives. 

Voice to Diabetes is an innovative tool that leverages AI to diagnose type 2 diabetes based on the analysis of a person's voice. Traditionally, Type 2 diabetes diagnosis involves invasive blood work, but this study introduces a non-invasive and efficient alternative. Voice to Diabetes harnesses AI and machine learning techniques to detect subtle vocal changes that are imperceptible to the human ear. By incorporating features like pitch and intensity changes, the tool successfully distinguishes between individuals with and without Type 2 diabetes. 

The study, published in a Mayo Clinic journal, involved 267 participants in India, with 192 without diabetes and 75 with a prior diagnosis. Using a smartphone app, participants recorded short voice clips multiple times daily for two weeks. We analyzed 18,465 recordings, identifying 14 different acoustic characteristics that led to the creation of a highly accurate diabetes identification tool. For women, changes in pitch and deviation from the average pitch were crucial, while for men, the diagnosis relied on variations in voice strength or amplitude. With an accuracy of up to 89% for women and 86% for men, this innovative approach could pave the way for an accurate, low-cost, and accessible test for diabetes.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Voice to Diabetes is an innovative AI tool leveraging mobile phone audio to non-invasively diagnose Type 2 diabetes by analyzing subtle vocal changes, offering an accessible alternative to traditional blood tests.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Individuals in India at risk of or currently living with undiagnosed Type 2 diabetes, especially those facing barriers to traditional testing due to distance, cost, or resources, who have access to mobile phones.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: The campaign would focus on mobile app distribution channels and public health awareness initiatives, potentially utilizing audio-centric media like radio or mobile audio ads, to promote the availability and benefits of the diagnostic tool.
ai_enrichment.objective: The objective is to drive the adoption and utilization of the Voice to Diabetes mobile-based AI diagnostic tool to improve early detection of Type 2 diabetes in India.

content.description: Black Noise Festival in Matra Mountain hosts subculture artists missing from the bill of major festivals. Our technologically endorsed campaign was true to this spirit and successfully represented the 'genius loci'. Most alternative music blogs and cultural magazines reviewed the campaign. Fans of the festival and its performers also joined the choir on social media. We also set off a competition for aspiring composers to win festival tickets and experience the real Black Noise at the festival. Not only did the festival's core community praise it, but it was recognised by people all over the region, extending the festival by one day.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: The campaign used a technologically-endorsed audio craft approach to embody the unique subculture spirit and 'genius loci' of the Black Noise Festival, which spotlights artists overlooked by mainstream events.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Fans of alternative and subculture music, aspiring composers, and the existing community of the Black Noise Festival, primarily within the Matra Mountain region.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: The campaign was executed through an innovative audio craft experience, promoted via alternative music blogs, cultural magazines, and social media, and featured a competition for aspiring composers.
ai_enrichment.objective: The objective was to enhance brand recognition and engagement for the Black Noise Festival across the region, leading to its expansion and increased attendance.

content.description:
When you think of Spotify ads, what comes to mind? Ads in between songs? During podcasts? It’s safe to say that video ads aren’t your first thought. And that has consequences for our ad revenue. Media planners bucket us as an audio-only platform, an incremental element  in the larger media plan.

So, to make Spotify’s video ad offerings unforgettable, we hacked the most analytical part of the media planning experience: the RFP spreadsheet. We created Spreadbeats, the first-ever music video coded inside an Excel spreadsheet. Millions of data points transformed into a stunning music video, proving that Spotify’s video ads can break through the best.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: To demonstrate Spotify's powerful video advertising capabilities and challenge its audio-only perception among media professionals, the campaign created 'Spreadbeats,' the first-ever music video coded entirely within an Excel spreadsheet.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Media planners, media buyers, and advertising professionals responsible for media allocation who currently perceive Spotify primarily as an audio-only platform.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: The campaign created 'Spreadbeats,' a unique music video entirely coded within an Excel spreadsheet using millions of data points, designed as a branded entertainment piece to be presented to and shared with media planning decision-makers.
ai_enrichment.objective: To shift media planners' perception of Spotify from an audio-only platform to a viable video advertising destination, increasing consideration for its video ad offerings and driving ad revenue.

Magnetic Stories transforms the audio of scary Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) machines, which can be louder than a military jet, into beautiful children’s audiobooks.

With Magnetic Stories, Siemens Healthineers, a top MRI manufacturer, changes the way that hospitals, radiologists, parents, and children interact with their product. It is no longer a giant, cold, scary machine. Instead, it is a gateway to a delightful experience for children, allowing pediatric patients to immerse themselves in original stories during their scans, all while easing their fears about the MRI machines and contributing to their overall health journey.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Magnetic Stories transforms the loud, intimidating sounds of MRI machines into calming and engaging audiobooks for children undergoing scans, creating a more positive and less frightening experience.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Hospitals, radiologists, and parents of pediatric patients, with the ultimate goal of improving the experience for pediatric patients undergoing MRI scans.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: The campaign creates original children's audiobooks, utilizing real MRI sounds, which are integrated as branded entertainment directly into the MRI scanning experience for pediatric patients.
ai_enrichment.objective: To transform the perception of MRI machines from scary to delightful, improve the patient experience for pediatric scans, and ease fears associated with medical imaging.

The Irish Heritage Council offers protected status, tax support and other financial benefits to places of historical significance that are open to the public, such as museums. Heineken’s Pub Museums turns historic Irish pubs into virtual museums, making them eligible for tax reductions, helping their rich past safeguard their future.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Heineken's "Pub Museums" initiative transforms struggling historic Irish pubs into virtual museums, enabling them to gain protected status and financial benefits from the Irish Heritage Council to safeguard their future.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Historic Irish pub owners and operators, as well as Irish adults who value local heritage, culture, and traditional pub experiences.

ai_enrichment.objective: The objective is to enhance Heineken's brand reputation and affinity through a strong Corporate Social Responsibility initiative, demonstrating commitment to Irish culture and supporting its business partners.

content.description: The Heaviest Jersey turned a business problem into a brand experience. While many brands adopt leaks as part of their strategy to launch football kits, São Paulo F.C. can't do that because their kits are always almost the same (due to statute restrictions). So The Heaviest Jersey repurposed the leaking culture, by shifting its focus from the jersey design to the jersey history. Leaking not only the new kit, but everything it carries with it: 94 years of history, resilience, overcoming difficulties, glories and conquests. Creating a 1.2TB jersey image impossible to download, but also impossible to ignore.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: New Balance subverted the common football kit leak strategy by "leaking" the immense 94-year history and legacy of the São Paulo F.C. jersey, rather than its design, embodied in an undownloadable 1.2TB digital image.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: São Paulo F.C. supporters and football enthusiasts who appreciate club history and innovative brand storytelling.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: The core tactic involved creating and promoting a massive 1.2TB digital image of the jersey, impossible to download but designed to spread virally as "leaked" content across digital and entertainment platforms.
ai_enrichment.objective: To build brand affinity and generate widespread buzz for New Balance and São Paulo F.C. by uniquely highlighting the club's deep history and legacy.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: A spectacular live event designed to officially launch the Olympic Games Paris 2024, showcasing French culture and universal Olympic values to a global audience.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Global citizens of all ages interested in sports, culture, major international events, and national pride, as well as general entertainment viewers worldwide.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A grand live spectacle broadcast globally across major television networks and digital streaming platforms, supported by extensive social media engagement and traditional media coverage.
ai_enrichment.objective: To officially inaugurate the Olympic Games Paris 2024, generate global excitement and viewership, and promote the host city's culture while reinforcing the Olympic spirit.

content.description: Inspired by a true story, 47 is Café Joyeux’s completely hand-made, animated stop-motion film created not just for, but by the Down syndrome community. Every aspect, from music, to voiceover, to character design is created with individuals with and touched by Down syndrome. Our film follows the life of Robert, a man with Down syndrome. Rejected by others as a child, he embarks on a personal journey symbolized by a raft. Along the way, he faces challenges that represent different life stages. In these scenarios, he recognizes that the world simply isn't ready to recognize his greatness and continues his odyssey.  Finally, at age 47, he finds acceptance and purpose at Café  Joyeux, a place where the world finally embraces his extraordinary abilities. The film is a heartwarming tale of resilience, acceptance, and inclusion.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: 47 is a hand-made, stop-motion animated film, created by and for the Down syndrome community, narrating Robert's lifelong journey to find acceptance and purpose, ultimately discovering it at Café Joyeux at age 47.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Individuals interested in disability inclusion, social impact, and heartwarming stories, as well as families of individuals with Down syndrome and supporters of socially conscious brands.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A branded stop-motion animated film, fully developed by individuals with and touched by Down syndrome, distributed as branded entertainment and content.
ai_enrichment.objective: To build brand affinity and awareness for Café Joyeux as a place of acceptance and purpose, highlighting its social mission and inclusive values.

content.description: It's very easy to remain incognito in online video games, and anyone can pretend to be someone else for purposes that can harm minors. In Mexico, 81.4% of gamers play on mobile devices. Thousands of children are being recruited through mobile video games to join organized crime. These children become easy prey, being contacted through the most popular and widely played games like GTA, Free Fire, among others. Movistar Telecom is a socially responsible brand in the digital environment, concerned about providing gamers with a safe space. That's why we created an online video game film to connect and deliver a message based on real events. Today, the danger is no longer just on the streets; it's on our mobile devices.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Movistar created a unique online video game film to dramatically portray the real-world danger of organized crime recruiting minors through popular mobile games, emphasizing that the threat now resides on mobile devices.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Parents and guardians in Mexico concerned about their children's online safety, as well as gamers and the general public, particularly those who play or have children who play mobile video games.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: The core tactic was the creation and distribution of an "online video game film" that uses real-event narratives to educate and warn about the dangers of child recruitment within mobile gaming environments.
ai_enrichment.objective: To raise public awareness about the urgent threat of organized crime recruiting minors through mobile video games and position Movistar as a socially responsible brand committed to digital safety.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: KFC Thailand introduces a new product line of localized rice bowls, presented through engaging branded entertainment that personifies KFC as a friendly 'Uncle' to connect with Thai consumers.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Thai consumers of all ages, particularly those seeking convenient, satisfying meals that blend familiar local flavors with the trusted taste of KFC.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: The campaign likely deployed a hero film or a series of engaging content pieces featuring the 'Uncle KFC' persona across digital channels (e.g., YouTube, Facebook) and potentially traditional media like TV, along with in-store branding.
ai_enrichment.objective: To generate awareness and drive trial and sales for KFC Thailand's new localized rice bowl product line, while enhancing the brand's local connection and appeal.

content.description: This is the story of how McDonald's, an iconic brand nearly 70 years old, broke from tradition to embrace its anime alter ego, WcDonald’s. The fan-to-fan strategy, taps into subcultures our fans care about and their fandom for the McDonald’s brand. WcDonald’s was the next iteration – reimagining McDonald’s by creating a fully immersive anime world across multiple touchpoints. From packaging and store design to manga on bags and anime spots, every element invited fans to explore and share their fandom. This world-building experience encouraged young adults to visit McDonald’s, offering them an escape into the anime universe.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: McDonald's embraced its anime alter ego, WcDonald's, creating a fully immersive anime world across multiple brand touchpoints to engage fans.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Young adults who are fans of both anime subculture and the McDonald's brand.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: The campaign involved transforming packaging, store designs, featuring manga on bags, and creating anime video spots.
ai_enrichment.objective: To encourage young adults to visit McDonald's by offering an immersive escape into an anime universe.

So we set out to pay tribute to a fellow icon of nausea with The Last Barf Bag, an entire campaign built around giving the barf bag a grand farewell. We launched with a documentary that explores every corner of the barf bag universe and premiered the film at a museum exhibit that literally put barf bags on a pedestal. We also designed an e-commerce platform that imagines a better future by selling repurposed bags for everything but barf—all to show people the impact and efficacy of Dramamine in an unexpected way.

When you think of Spotify ads, what comes to mind? Ads in between songs? During podcasts? It’s safe to say that video ads aren’t your first thought. And that has consequences for our ad revenue. Media planners bucket us as an audio-only platform, an incremental element  in the larger media plan.

So, to make Spotify’s video ad offerings unforgettable, we hacked the most analytical part of the media planning experience: the RFP spreadsheet. We created Spreadbeats, the first-ever music video coded inside an Excel spreadsheet. Millions of data points transformed into a stunning music video, proving that Spotify’s video ads can break through the best.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: To prove Spotify's innovative video ad capabilities, the campaign created "Spreadbeats," the first-ever music video coded entirely within an Excel RFP spreadsheet, transforming millions of data points into a visual spectacle.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Media planners, advertisers, and marketing decision-makers responsible for allocating media budgets and evaluating ad platforms in a B2B context.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: The campaign executed a unique creative activation by developing "Spreadbeats," a groundbreaking music video embedded and coded entirely within an interactive Excel RFP spreadsheet, which was then presented to target media professionals.
ai_enrichment.objective: To dramatically shift the perception of Spotify from an audio-only platform to a formidable video ad platform, thereby increasing consideration and adoption of its video ad offerings among media planners.

content.description:
Lap of Legends redefined sports entertainment by blending cutting-edge technology and storytelling to create the first-ever real-vs.-virtual Formula 1 race. Starring Logan Sargeant at the iconic Silverstone Circuit, the project brought to life augmented reality avatars of legendary F1 drivers who raced against Logan in real time. Powered by advanced AI and machine learning, the virtual competitors were designed using 45 years of historical race data and 42.3 million data points, ensuring their driving styles and strategies mirrored their real-world counterparts.

Using real-time telemetry from Logan’s car, the digital avatars responded dynamically to his every move, creating an authentic and immersive race experience. The production captured this groundbreaking event with 28 unique camera angles—more than any F1 broadcast—providing viewers with an unprecedented perspective on the action. The film combined high-fidelity visuals, seamless AR integration, and cinematic storytelling to deliver an experience that was as exhilarating as it was innovative.

Lap of Legends was not just a race; it was a spectacle that bridged the gap between the physical and digital worlds. By leveraging state-of-the-art technology, Michelob ULTRA created an immersive narrative that resonated with fans globally, setting a new benchmark for innovation in sports entertainment.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: Michelob ULTRA created the first-ever real-vs.-virtual Formula 1 race, where Logan Sargeant competed against AI-powered augmented reality avatars of F1 legends, using historical data and real-time telemetry to simulate an authentic race experience.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: F1 enthusiasts, sports fans, and tech-savvy adults aged 21-45 who appreciate innovative entertainment and premium beer brands like Michelob ULTRA.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A groundbreaking branded entertainment event was produced as the core content, featuring a real-vs.-virtual F1 race captured with 28 unique cameras, likely distributed as a hero film or series across digital and potentially broadcast platforms.
ai_enrichment.objective: To enhance brand image, drive engagement among sports and F1 fans, and redefine sports entertainment through technological innovation and storytelling.

content.description:
Individuals with intellectual disabilities, like autism and Down syndrome, face disproportionate barriers in accessing meaningful employment opportunities. In France, individuals with intellectual disabilities are two to three times more affected by unemployment than the rest of the population, with only 0.5 percent having a job. In the US, over seven million people have mental or cognitive disabilities, and 80% of them are unemployed. Recognizing this glaring inequality, Café Joyeux was established with a global mission: to transform society's perception of disability by dedicating its efforts to the employment and training of people with mental and cognitive disabilities. With 18 locations across Europe, Café Joyeux has recently expanded its reach, opening its first shop in Manhattan, NY.

Inspired by a true story, 47 is Café Joyeux’s completely hand-made, animated stop-motion film created not just for, but by the Down syndrome community. Every aspect, from music, to voiceover, to character design is created with individuals with and touched by Down syndrome. Our film follows the life of Robert, a man with Down syndrome. Rejected by others as a child, he embarks on a personal journey symbolized by a raft. Along the way, he faces challenges that represent different life stages. In these scenarios, he recognizes that the world simply isn't ready to recognize his greatness and continues his odyssey.  Finally, at age 47, he finds acceptance and purpose at Café  Joyeux, a place where the world finally embraces his extraordinary abilities. The film is a heartwarming tale of resilience, acceptance, and inclusion. 

The craft of the film was developed with the community. Robert’s character was crafted by an artist, who is a father to a child with Down syndrome, in collaboration with his daughter. The design of the secondary characters reinforces that we are seeing the world through Robert’s eyes. Manipulating the proportions of characters, such as elongating their arms and introducing peculiar traits like masked faces, emphasized that the depicted scenarios, along with what was presented to Robert within them, were unjust and hindered his true potential from shining through.

Colors also play a significant role in the story, with the yellow scarf symbolizing both Café Joyeux’s brand color and Robert’s mother's words of encouragement that guide him through his journey, and are reflective of the company's ethos of believing in oneself.  The entire odyssey takes place in his imagination, and uses a mix of warm and colder tones, reflecting Robert's resilience in approaching the challenges of colder, solitary moments with a positive and warm mindset.

We needed a song that embodied the emotional odyssey of our main character. The song “Carry On” by Grammy-award-winning FUN captures the intimacy of Robert’s journey while also demonstrating his confidence throughout. To bring the voice of the community through every stage of production, we re-recorded “Carry On” with talented musicians from across the Down syndrome community, José Omar Davila and Sujeet Desai, with FUN’s lead singer Nate Ruess’ powerful vocal delivery.

The film increased Café Joyeux job applications and brand recognition, while helping in-store traffic grow exponentially.

ai_enrichment.target_audience: Conscious consumers, especially those in Europe and New York City, who value social responsibility and inclusive employment, along with disability advocates and potential partners.

content.description:
Every ad PEDIGREE makes around the world has one universal similarity. It always features a dog in it. We wondered, what if that dog could be adoptable. Partnering with Nexus Studios, we’ve developed a bespoke AI system that can transform a single image of a shelter dog into studio quality photography. This 'glow up' image is then mapped to a CGI rig, allowing the photo-real double to be positioned in any pose to suit any piece of PEDIGREE creative.   

From main meal, treat and retail ads, every dog we feature can now be a real shelter dog who needs a home.   

Adoptable places local shelter dogs into the ads you see, and when that dog is adopted it’s instantly dropped out of media rotation. The Adoptable initiative supercharges our brand purpose. Now every dollar we spend on media doesn’t just take our products to the world, it takes shelter dogs, too.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: PEDIGREE leverages a bespoke AI and CGI system to transform single images of shelter dogs into studio-quality photography, allowing every dog featured in their global advertising to be a real, adoptable shelter dog.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Pet owners, potential dog adopters, and the general public who consume PEDIGREE advertisements across various media.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: Integrating AI-enhanced images of local shelter dogs into all PEDIGREE advertisements globally, including main meal, treat, and retail ads, with dynamic rotation to remove dogs once adopted.
ai_enrichment.objective: To supercharge PEDIGREE's brand purpose by seamlessly integrating dog adoption into its global advertising strategy, enhancing brand image and facilitating pet adoptions.

content.description:

Well over 10 million gifts ended up in landfills in 2023 and whilst Philips is trying to reduce their environmental impact by refurbishing returned products, the initiative is aimed at highlighting the problems within society and transforming the way people buy and how retailers sell to consumers. 


The creative thinking behind the campaign focuses on the tension between ‘new equals better’ and ‘better than new’. Responding to the needs of an increasing number of consumers, and building on the company’s commitment to sustainability and driving a more circular economy, Philips’ goal is to make products that can be refurbished and reused.

ai_enrichment.target_audience: Environmentally conscious consumers and those open to sustainable consumption models, aged 25-55, who are willing to consider refurbished alternatives to new products.

content.description:
Around the world, locals are increasingly frustrated with mass tourism and visitors’ behaviour. During peak season, protests erupted in several European cities, with tensions running so high in Barcelona that some residents even resorted to spray-ing tourists with water guns.
 
Recognising the need for a different approach, Copenhagen took a more thoughtful path.

We transformed the way visitors experience Copenhagen by turning SUSTAINABILITY into a CURRENCY. The city introduced a unique system where Copenhagen attractions rewarded climate-friendly actions. Tourists were encouraged to take sustainable transportation or participate in other green deeds such as joining cleanup projects in return for free experiences, entry to museums or vegetarian food. This approach turned contributing to the city's wellbeing and environmental health into an attraction in itself, becoming a central aspect of the Copenhagen brand experience, during tourism’s high season making tourism more sustainable.

CopenPay became the biggest high-season tourism news story globally, covered by CNN, Le Figaro, The New York Times, BBC, Der Spiegel, The Guardian, Times of In-dia, China Today and over 4800 news articles. 111 million dollars in earned media. 
 
More than 76.000 CopenPay users contributed to a win-win-win scenario.
 
Win for the environment and the city:
•	+29% sustainable transportation
•	1,200 kg of trash collected
•	Thousands of hours of volunteer work
 
Win for the tourists:
•	100% of tourists were satisfied with their experience
•	98% would most likely recommend it to others

Win for the attractions:
•	All attractions saw an increase in visitors
 
Given its success, CopenPay will now be rolled more widely.

ai_enrichment.concept_summary: CopenPay transforms the visitor experience in Copenhagen by turning sustainability into a currency, rewarding tourists with free experiences and attractions for engaging in climate-friendly actions like sustainable transportation or cleanup projects.
ai_enrichment.target_audience: Tourists visiting Copenhagen, particularly those conscious of their environmental impact or seeking responsible ways to explore the city.
ai_enrichment.execution_tactics: A city-wide rewards system implemented through Copenhagen attractions, incentivizing specific sustainable tourist behaviors with free entry, experiences, or food.
ai_enrichment.objective: To encourage sustainable tourism practices, mitigate the negative impacts of mass tourism, and improve visitor-local relations within Copenhagen.

content.description: Did you know 1 in 12 sheep is gay? Since they won’t breed, these rams are often killed. With wool prices at an all-time low, we found a way to create a new revenue stream for Sheep Farm Stücke and help gay sheep and gay people at the same time: Rainbow Wool is the first gay textile thread for queer rights worldwide. We rescued gay rams, giving them a safe space to live, love and grow wool. Their wool is crafted into fashion and was launched by German queer icon and Tokio Hotel frontman Bill Kaulitz, helping spark a global conversation about equality.  All profits fund global LGBTQ+ charities.
